title: Lesson 77: (Grammar) - indicates direct object of action; indicates subje...

intro_cards.title: Object Marker: を
intro_cards.body:
を (pronounced 'o') marks the direct object — what the action is done to.

• ご飯を食べます。(I eat rice.)
• 本を読みます。(I read a book.)
• 音楽を聴きます。(I listen to music.)

を also marks space through which movement occurs:
• 橋を渡ります。(I cross over the bridge.)
• 公園を歩きます。(I walk through the park.)

Note: を is often dropped in casual speech but is standard in polite and written Japanese.
